summary: Addressed systems_architect REQUEST_CHANGES for SIM-001: validateWorldStateV0 now rejects malformed nested unknown input with path-specific invalid-schema errors without throwing, runtime state tables now enforce duplicate-row and exact definition coverage invariants, and architecture guard/fixture coverage now proves DOM global, Node fs/path IO, real-time API, and render-frame API violations.

tests_run.result: Vitest ran tests/sim-core-world-state.test.ts: 1 file passed, 9 tests passed.
Architecture boundaries passed. Self-test fixture produced 11 expected violation(s).
architecture:check passed; sim determinism matched Node and Worker-compatible hash 61e0ba1c; root Vitest 2 files/11 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file/3 tests passed.

decisions: Kept fixes limited to SIM-001 allowed paths and did not edit orchestration/status/route files.
validateWorldStateV0 now uses an internal structurally checked candidate type before semantic checks, avoiding unsafe malformed entry access.
Runtime state exact coverage is enforced for polities, persons, districts, settlements, and routes.
risks: SIM-001 remains R3 and requires systems_architect review before acceptance.
The worktree still contains project coordination/status/route/outbox changes outside this fix scope; they were not modified by this pass.
requested_action: Review the SIM-001 REQUEST_CHANGES fixes: malformed unknown input structural safety, runtime table exact coverage invariants, and strengthened architecture/static guard coverage. Return ACCEPT, REQUEST_CHANGES, or BLOCK.
